item.title: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showing customers other users' transactions
item.description: The Lloyds Banking Group customers reported being able to view payments and charges from other sources.

item.title: Iran war causing ‘largest disruption in history’ to oil supplies, says IEA
item.description: Global crude output will fall to lowest level in four years, energy agency warns in report

item.title: Minister defends PM's handling of Mandelson appointment
item.description: Documents show Sir Keir Starmer was warned the peer's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein posed a "reputational risk".

item.title: My phone contents were shared with the police colleague I accused of rape
item.description: Police Scotland has been fined £66,000 by a watchdog after it failed to protect sensitive personal information.

item.title: Draper fights to 'mind-blowing' win over Djokovic
item.description: Jack Draper produces a superb fightback to beat Novak Djokovic and reach the Indian Wells fourth round - a result he believes could be "a real big moment" in his season.

item.title: Hizbollah’s war with Israel deepens its isolation in Lebanon
item.description: Widespread opposition to conflict and mass displacement stoke sectarian tensions

item.title: Supertankers rush to Red Sea port as Iran war chokes Gulf oil exports
item.description: Saudi pipeline allows ships to avoid perilous Strait of Hormuz but they must now brave notorious hotspot for Houthi attacks

item.title: The AI-driven ‘kill chain’ transforming how the US wages war
item.description: Systems from Palantir and Anthropic are helping to turn torrents of battlefield data into thousands of strikes

item.title: Military briefing: the closest US-Israel war yet
item.description: Not since the Suez Crisis has the IDF fought alongside an ally in a major military offensive

item.title: Which chokepoint wins in a game of geoeconomic Top Trumps?
item.description: It depends on its damage to the target, potential for blowback and durability

item.title: 'It was the stuff of dreams' - Liza Tarbuck leaves Radio 2 Saturday show
item.description: Shaun Keaveny, host of The Rock Show, will stand in for her until the end of the end of the month.

item.title: Oil continues to rise despite deal to release record amount of reserves
item.description: It comes as Iranian attacks on ships intensify in the crucial Strait of Hormuz waterway.

item.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

item.description: The Treasury Committee will look at whether "the goalposts [have] been moved in a way which is unfair".

item.title: Hundreds of GPs tell BBC they have never refused a sick note over mental health concerns
item.description: The number of fit notes issued has been rising, with more than 11.2m approved in England last year.

item.title: Chris Mason: Some nuggets but no huge revelations in first batch of Mandelson files
item.description: This first digital document drop about the prime minister's decision to appoint Lord Mandelson as US ambassador is interesting, but not explosive.

item.title:
TRUMP PREPARES NEW TARIFF INVESTIGATIONS
The Office of the United States Trade Representative is preparing to launch Section 301 investigations into alleged unfair trade practices by multiple countries. The probes could lead to **new tariffs on nations including China and possibly the European Union.
The investigations would replace the temporary 10% global tariffs imposed last month after the Supreme Court of the United States ruled several earlier levies illegal.
The probes may target issues such as industrial overcapacity, digital trade rules, and forced labor practices, though the process could take months before tariffs are imposed.
item.description:
TRUMP PREPARES NEW TARIFF INVESTIGATIONS

The Office of the United States Trade Representative is preparing to launch Section 301 investigations into alleged unfair trade practices by multiple countries. The probes could lead to **new tariffs on nations including China and possibly the European Union.

The investigations would replace the temporary 10% global tariffs imposed last month after the Supreme Court of the United States ruled several earlier levies illegal.

The probes may target issues such as industrial overcapacity, digital trade rules, and forced labor practices, though the process could take months before tariffs are imposed.
